Conversely, divine revelation and the promise of pure, eternal fulfillment in Paradise serve as the antidote and motivation for believers to resist these temptations and purify their hearts."

"explanation": "**The Satanic Instigation of Illicit Desires**
Ibn Kathir elucidates that Shaytan actively seeks to mislead humanity by "arousing false desires" (وَلأُمَنِّيَنَّهُمْ) and making deceptive promises. This instigation is a primary mechanism through which individuals deviate from divine guidance, leading to spiritual loss in both this life and the Hereafter. Shaytan's objective is to secure a "fixed and known share" of humanity for misguidance, tempting them with feigned repentance and procrastination in righteous deeds, ultimately leading to a manifest loss.

**Manifestations of Worldly Lusts and Shirk**
The tafsir highlights various forms of illicit desires. In the narrative of Bilqis,

"explanation": "**Context and Significance**
This verse serves as a profound admonition to humanity, particularly addressing the contemporaries of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, by highlighting the transient nature of worldly allurements. Al-Qurtubi notes that Allah enumerates four primary categories of wealth, each representing a distinct source of trial for various societal groups: gold and silver for merchants, horses for rulers, livestock for nomadic peoples, and cultivated land for landowners. Women and children, however, represent universal trials that transcend these specific classifications.

**The Adorner of Desires**
Scholarly discourse exists regarding the agent responsible for "beautifying" these desires (زُيِّنَ). 'Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb held the view that Allah Himself is the Adorner, implying a divinely ordained inclination within human nature. Conversely, al-Ḥasan posited that Shayṭān is the adoring agent, achieving this through insidious whispers and deceit. Al-Qurtubi reconciles these perspectives by suggesting that Allah's adornment is through creation, while Shayṭān's is through temptation, thereby clarifying the dualistic influence on human desires. The term *shahawāt* (appetites) is the plural of *shahwah*, encompassing all forms of human desire.

**Specific Worldly Allurements**
Women are explicitly mentioned first due to their profound capacity as a primary temptation and a significant "snare of Shayṭān," indicating the heightened nature of this trial. *Banūn* (children), the plural of *ibn*, represent another significant trial, often leading individuals to excessive accumulation of wealth for their progeny, fostering traits such as cowardice, miserliness, and sorrow. The phrase *al-qanāṭīr al-muqanṭarah* refers to "heaped-up mounds of gold and silver." *Qanāṭīr* is the plural of *qinṭār*, a term denoting a substantial quantity of wealth, though its precise measure remains a subject of scholarly disagreement among exegetes, with various estimations ranging from 1200 *ūqiyyahs* to a bull's hide full of gold. *Muqanṭarah* signifies multiplication, minting, or completeness. Linguistically, *dhahab* (gold) is derived from *dhahāb* (departure), and *fiḍḍah* (silver) from *infaḍḍa* (to be scattered), subtly hinting a

"arabic_text": "زُيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ حُبُّ ٱلشَّهَوَٰتِ مِنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ وَٱلْبَنِينَ وَٱلْقَنَـٰطِيرِ ٱلْمُقَنطَرَةِ مِنَ ٱلذَّهَبِ وَٱلْفِضَّةِ وَٱلْخَيْلِ ٱلْمُسَوَّمَةِ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ وَٱلْحَرْثِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ مَتَـٰعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَٱللَّهُ عِندَهُۥ حُسْنُ ٱلْمَـَٔابِ"

"text_saheeh_international": "Beautified for people is the love of that which they desire - of women and sons, heaped-up sums of gold and silver, fine branded horses, and cattle and tilled land. That is the enjoyment of worldly life, but Allah has with Him the best return.",

...urtubi explain that while these are natural inclinations and can serve beneficial purposes, they fundamentally represent the ephemeral 'enjoyment of worldly life.' The verse ultimately redirects the believer's focus towards the superior and eternal 'best return' that lies with Allah, emphasizing the critical importance of intention and the pursuit of the Hereafter over transient material gains."

textPayload: "arabic_text": "وَيَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ فَفَزِعَ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَن فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا مَن شَاءَ اللَّهُ ۚ وَكُلٌّ أَتَوْهُ دَاخِرِينَ"

"text_saheeh_international": "And [mention] the Day the Horn will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified, except whom Allah wills. And all will come to Him humbled.",
"arabic_text": "أَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا جَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ لِيَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ وَالنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ"
"text_saheeh_international": "Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest therein and the day enlightening? Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.",
"arabic_text": "وَوَقَعَ الْقَوْلُ عَلَيْهِم بِمَا ظَلَمُوا فَهُمْ لَا يَنطِقُونَ"
"text_saheeh_international": "And the decree will fall upon them for the wrong they have done, and they will not speak.",
"arabic_text": "حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءُوا قَالَ أَكَذَّبْتُم بِآيَاتِي وَلَمْ تُحِيطُوا بِهَا عِلْمًا أَمَّاذَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ"
"text_saheeh_international": "Until, when they arrive, He will say, \"Did you deny My signs while you had not encompassed them in knowledge, or what [else] were you doing?\"",
"arabic_text": "وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ فَوْجًا مِّمَّن يُكَذِّبُ بِآيَاتِنَا فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ"
"text_saheeh_international": "And [mention] the Day when We will gather from every nation a company from among those who denied Our signs, and they will be driven [in ranks]",
